name: MiruShin
subtitle: New way to watch and discover media.
description: Official AltStore / SideStore / Feather / LiveContainer source for MiruShin - a cross-platform anime streaming and player app.

apps.name: MiruShin
apps.subtitle: New way to watch and discover media.
apps.localizedDescription:
MiruShin takes the familiar feel of AnimeShin and pushes it further with a cleaner Flutter architecture, real Sora module support, cross-platform playback, and deeper AniList profile flows. It can switch between TMDB-driven discovery and AniList-driven catalog views, then carry that context into your library, watch flow, and player.

MiruShin is a media player and interface layer. It does not host or provide any content, and ships no built-in Sora modules.

versions.localizedDescription:
- Added an MSI installer for Windows (`.msi`), alongside the existing setup `.exe` and portable `.zip`. The MSI lets you choose a per-user or all-users install.
- Fixed Android TV D-pad navigation on the Home and Discovery screens: focus no longer skips the movie posters and jumps straight to the "see more" / "Load more" button, so you can now reach and select every poster.

- Fixed playback speed not being restored after the player restarts.
- Added a short delay before auto-playing the next episode after the current stream finishes.

- Added Clear All and Apply buttons to the source filter.
- Updated several localization strings.

- Added a new Sources page. You can open it from the Sources button on the Addons page.
- You can now add your own catalog by pasting a catalog URL, for example example.com/modules.json.
- You can open a source, browse its addons, and install them into your library with one tap.
- Addons that are already installed are now clearly marked.
- Added search by addon name, author, language, and type.
- Added filters for multiple languages and types at the same time, for example Russian + English or anime + movies.
- Added a Downloadable only filter.
- You can now refresh, rename, copy the URL, or remove a source.
- Removing a source will not remove your installed addons.
- Added support for adding addons by addon ID.

- TMDB season picker now shows the real number of seasons from the addon.
- Fixed watch progress not tracking for TMDB episodes in the local library, including across auto-next season changes.

- Fixed auto-next episode and watch progress for auto-played episodes.
- Previously, when an episode started automatically, watch progress was not saved and the next episode did not play correctly.
- Now every episode saves progress, marks itself as watched at 85%, and reliably continues to the next episode.
- Fixed the update download button sometimes not working.
- Fixed auto-progress and auto-next reliability issues.

- Android TV support with Leanback launcher/banner, TV-aware navigation, focus rings, and d-pad-friendly cards.
- Player remote fixes: OK play/pause, paused UI focus navigation, Settings/Quality/Episodes/Subtitles reachable by d-pad, sliders no longer trap focus, Back hides chrome first.
- Android TV WebView cursor improvements: smaller/smoother cursor, native tap fallback, better keyboard/textbox handling, and cursor support for YouTube trailers.
- AniList editor improvements from TV: hold OK on media cards opens entry edit actions where supported.
- Library/search/addon/settings text fields improved for d-pad escape.
- Playback fixes including seek/d-pad fixes, PiP handling fixes, iOS PiP fix, and player back-button fixes.
- AniList and Shikimori metadata/library fixes.

- Improved auto-next episode, mark as watched, and AniList sync.
- Fixed progress on finished streams, even when the stream jumps back to 0:00.
- Fixed player controls and mouse cursor not hiding after auto-next.
- Fixed buffering spinner sometimes staying visible after playback resumes.
- AniList now marks anime as Completed when you finish the last known episode.
- Fixed Trailer button, now it appears correctly on all devices.
- Moved Auto AniList progress setting, now it only shows in the AniList catalog.
- Fixed settings not saving after app restart.
- Poster card style and compact mode are now remembered.

- Added a built-in TMDB key, so MiruShin works out of the box.
- TMDB metadata now loads on first launch without setup.
- Added a Use custom API key option in Settings -> API Connections.
- You can still use your own TMDB Read Access Token if needed.
- TMDB metadata is now enabled by default.

- Improved video playback reliability.
- Fixed some streams not playing when proxy playback failed.
- The player now automatically tries another playback method when needed.
- Episodes in the TMDB Catalog are now sorted by season.
- Fixed WebView support on Windows. YouTube trailers now play correctly on Windows.
- Added trailer support for AniList and TMDB catalogs. The trailer button is shown only when a trailer is available.

- Improved the anime and manga detail pages with a cleaner and more polished layout.
- Improved the Edit button design and placement.
- AniList cards now always open the Edit page in AniList mode.
- Fixed Auto-Next so it now keeps the server selected inside the player. The next episode will resolve using the chosen server instead of falling back to the old or default one.
- Fixed an Auto-Next issue where the app could return to the Choose Episode page instead of continuing playback.

- Added pinch-out gesture support in the player to switch to Zoom mode.
- Added pinch-in gesture support to switch back to Normal mode.
- Improved the left sidebar behavior, so it now switches states earlier and feels more responsive.
- Reduced the height of the top and bottom black fog effects in the player UI for a cleaner viewing experience.
- Fixed player initialization: the video now repaints immediately instead of waiting for mouse movement to trigger a rebuild.

- Added a custom accent color picker.
- Removed unused options from Settings for a cleaner experience.

- Added a floating popup notification for new app updates.
- Improved mobile auto-next playback. The next episode now opens immediately in fullscreen player mode for a smoother watching experience.
- Updated Discord Rich Presence: the application name now appears as **MiruShin**.
- Fixed slow Choose Episode loading for custom/addon sources.
- Episodes from the addon now appear first, using the addon’s own episode name and poster when available.
- AniList/AniZip episode titles and thumbnails now load lazily only after the episode list is already shown.
- Improved support for custom Sora episode formats, including grouped episode lists and chapter-style APIs.
- Reduced stale search work when switching from Find Sources to Choose Episode.
- Auto-next now fetches the latest addon episode list instead of relying on old cached episode data.
- Added animated hover backgrounds for Windows PiP buttons, making the controls feel smoother and more responsive.

- Smarter Escape key in the player
- Infinite scroll on AniList Favourites

- Improved Picture-in-Picture support on Windows for a smoother and more native-like experience.
- Fixed the missing app icon in the Linux .deb package.

- Fixed the Linux crash when opening an episode, video playback works again.
- Added a `.deb` package for Debian/Ubuntu (AppImage and tar.gz still available).
- More stable Linux playback across releases.

- Added a second player engine for improved playback stability.
- Added PiP-style mini player support for Windows and Linux.
- Added ARB localization support for English, Russian, and Japanese.
- Added a local HLS proxy for the MPV player engine.

- Added startup page selection in Settings.
- Added skip-marker source selection in Player Settings.
- Improved iOS PiP exit handling so the player restores correctly.
- Fixed AniList sync for the Favorite heart button.
- Changed the default catalog mode to AniList.

- Added support for custom headers for subtitle tracks and network requests.
- Fixed the AniList favorites list and added a Load More button.
- Added a Windows installer for easier installation.

- Updated the player loading indicator to use a cleaner white circular spinner.
- Added timeline preview on the player progress slider.
- Fixed the Android splash screen logo display.

- Added airing anime notifications for supported platforms.
- Added favorites support for anime, manga, and edit pages.
- Added AniList status badges on Board and Discovery posters.
- Improved mobile double-tap seeking speed and feedback.
- Changed swipe seeking to use 2-second steps.
- Improved temporary speed badge position and styling.
- Fixed iOS PiP close cleanup so the player overlay clears correctly.
- Polished AniList edit dialog progress controls.
- Improved anime and manga tags.
- Added Edit action for Board and Discovery pages.

- Added Linux support
- Added a shortcut in Settings to open AniList settings

- Hold Space or press and hold the screen to speed up video playback
- Improved Discord Rich Presence with better playback details
- Fixed auto-next episodes so the correct episode name appears in the player
- MiruShin is now available as its first public release, bringing the core app experience with discovery, tracking, source selection, and playback in a desktop-friendly Flutter app.

apps.versionDescription:
- Added an MSI installer for Windows (`.msi`), alongside the existing setup `.exe` and portable `.zip`. The MSI lets you choose a per-user or all-users install.
- Fixed Android TV D-pad navigation on the Home and Discovery screens: focus no longer skips the movie posters and jumps straight to the "see more" / "Load more" button, so you can now reach and select every poster.
